training.quantization: nf4 4-bit with bf16 compute dtype, double quant

results.loss_descent_endpoints: 5.3597 -> 0.7295 (descent confirms gradient flow + LoRA + 4-bit cohort works end-to-end)

generation_smoke.prompt_user: Verdict for react 18.2.0 (no install scripts, popular)?
generation_smoke.comment: After 20 SFT steps on 5 synthetic dossier-verdict pairs the model emits a syntactically valid audit-report-shaped JSON verdict for a held-out package. Validates the chat-template + LoRA + 4-bit forward + decode path.

spend.breakdown_estimate: 0.28 burnt on first run (transformers/peft version skew), 0.33 on the successful run

lessons_learned: Single-GPU H100 inventory on vast.ai was paper-thin at this hour; widened filter to H100/H200/A100 single-GPU. Smoke does not require H100 specifically (1.5B model fits trivially on any 40GB+ card).
Vast.ai shows stale asks in /bundles/ that fail at /asks/<id>/ create with no_such_ask 404. Retry-across-offers needed.
pip --quiet hides resolver decisions. Explicit pinned versions catch the transformers/peft GenerationMixin import break before training starts.
PyTorch 2.4 + transformers 4.46 + peft 0.13 + trl 0.12 + accelerate 1.0.1 + bitsandbytes 0.44.1 is a known-good cohort for the Qwen2.5-Coder SFT-LoRA path on A100/H100.
ready_for_leonardo_notes: Saturday's Leonardo allocation on the 64x H100 nodes runs the same SFT-LoRA path that produced this 5.36 -> 0.73 loss descent on a single A100. Pipeline mechanics confirmed: bf16 + 4-bit base + LoRA r=16 alpha=32 + gradient_checkpointing(use_reentrant=False) all interoperate with the trl 0.12 SFTTrainer + processing_class= kwarg.
